channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Judge Declares Mistrial in Frank Stronach's Sexual Assault Case
item.description: A lawyer for billionaire businessman Frank Stronach has confirmed that a judge has declared a mistrial in his sexual assault conviction, overturning a previous guilty verdict. The ruling came unexpect...

item.title: DJ Josh Fawaz credits AI for 'Like a Prayer' cover
item.description: The article discusses DJ Josh Fawaz's decision to acknowledge artificial intelligence as a collaborator on his cover of the iconic Whitney Houston song 'Like a Prayer'. This move has sparked conversat...

item.title: Survey Reveals 41% of UK Adults Haven't Listened to a Full Album in a Year
item.description: A recent survey conducted by National Rail reveals that 41% of UK adults have not listened to a full album in the past year, highlighting a shift in music consumption habits. The Track Reset campaign,...

item.title: Motorcycle Taxi Riders Push for Safety Measures Like ID Checks and SOS Buttons
item.description: Motorcycle taxi riders in the Philippines are advocating for enhanced safety protocols, including passenger identity verification and an SOS button feature in ride-hailing apps, following a series of ...

item.title: Judge Orders Hearing on States' Request to Block Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger
item.description: A federal judge heard arguments from a coalition of states seeking to block the Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery merger, citing antitrust concerns over market concentration in theatrical and cable ind...

item.title: South African Police Arrest 69 During Anti-Immigration Protests
item.description: South African police arrested 69 people after violent anti-immigrant protests turned chaotic in Jeffreys Bay, Eastern Cape. The protests, organized by anti-migrant group March and March, led to lootin...

item.title: CDA Accuses INEC of Delaying Party Registration Despite Court Order
item.description: The Citizens Democratic Alliance (CDA) has accused the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) of ignoring a Federal High Court order to register the group as a political party. In a statemen...

item.title: Lucas Herbert leads Open Championship after historic 62, Sam Burns also records 62
item.description: Australian golfer Lucas Herbert came agonisingly close to becoming the first player to score a 61 in a professional tournament, finishing with a 62 at the Open Championship. South African-born America...

item.title: Tanzania's Largest Automotive Showdown Set to Launch in Arusha
item.description: On July 17, 2026, Arusha is set to host Tanzania’s most significant automotive business showcase, marking a pivotal moment for the country’s vehicle industry. The event, organized by local and interna...

item.title: Jackie Cameron's Culinary School Students Excel After Intense Training
item.description: The article highlights the remarkable transformation of students at Jackie Cameron’s School of Food and Wine over 18 months. Starting as clumsy and inexperienced, learners face challenges like cutting...

item.title: Philippines Demands Removal of Racist China Daily Video Depicting Filipinos as Monkeys
item.description: The article highlights the Philippines' diplomatic protest against a China Daily video that depicted Filipinos as monkeys, urging Beijing to remove it. The Department of Foreign Affairs criticized the...

item.title: Davido Supports Israel DMW During Father's Burial, Fans Praise His Loyalty
item.description: Afrobeats superstar Davido made headlines by arriving in Nigeria on a private jet to attend the burial of his aide Israel DMW's father, sparking widespread admiration online. The heartwarming airport ...

item.title: Ontario Premier Urges U.S. Politicians to Act on Wildfire Smoke, Cites Past Aid
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford has criticized U.S. politicians for complaining about wildfire smoke drifting across the border, urging them to act by sending aid similar to Canada's past assistance during ...

item.title: Botswana's Gold Reserves Boost Economic Stability and Assets
item.description: The news article highlights how Botswana's gold reserves are playing a crucial role in enhancing the country's economic stability and asset base. It emphasizes the strategic importance of these reserv...

item.title: Three-year-old boy recovering after crocodile attack at zoo
item.description: A three-year-old boy who was attacked by a crocodile in a zoo enclosure is making remarkable progress, according to his parents. The incident occurred on 18 June at Johnsons of Old Hurst near Huntingd...

item.title: Luxury Hotels Elevate Venice's Opulence with Stunning New Accommodations
item.description: The article highlights three new luxury hotels in Venice that redefine opulence, offering guests a blend of historical architecture and modern amenities. The Airelles Palladio, owned by French billion...

item.title: Myths and Real-World Locations Intersect in Ancient Maps of Odysseus's Journey
item.description: The article explores how ancient maps blended myths with real-world geography, focusing on Homer's Odyssey. It discusses how scholars have debated the locations of mythical places like Circe's island ...

item.title: Toronto Man Injured in Scooter Accident After Being Struck by Vehicle
item.description: A man was rushed to the hospital in critical condition following a collision involving a vehicle and a scooter in Toronto. The incident, reported on July 17, 2026, highlights the dangers of urban traf...

item.title: SpaceX and Pentagon Discuss Potential AI Collaboration
item.description: The article discusses ongoing negotiations between SpaceX, the aerospace manufacturer led by Elon Musk, and the U.S. Department of Defense regarding a potential collaboration on artificial intelligenc...

item.title: Gold's historical medical use and modern misconceptions
item.description: The article explores the controversial topic of gold injections, linking it to the tragic death of fitness influencer Connor Murphy. It explains how gold once had a legitimate place in medicine, with ...
